name: Bus Glue
explanation: A VCA-style stereo bus compressor for the classic mix-glue character. A feed-forward, RMS-ish level detector watches the summed program and drives a smooth soft-knee gain reduction computed in the dB domain. The same gain is applied to both channels (stereo-linked) so the image stays put while the mix tightens and punches. Three switchable ratios (2:1 / 4:1 / 10:1) plus Attack and Release shape the gain-reduction envelope; Makeup restores level and Mix blends in the dry signal for parallel glue. Loud, dense material is pulled together more than quiet passages, gluing a mix into a cohesive whole. Controls: Threshold, Ratio, Attack, Release, Makeup and Mix.
